framework_selection.reasoning: Paper is IBM hardware / quantum circuit based. Qiskit is the canonical IBM quantum SDK. No neural network weights — no PyTorch needed. SVM solved classically with sklearn. Scipy needed for SPSA and QP fallback. NumPy for all matrix ops.
framework_selection.simulation_backend: qiskit_aer.AerSimulator (statevector_method for ideal; aer_simulator for noisy)
framework_selection.config_system: YAML (PyYAML) + dataclasses — no Hydra needed for this scale

src/qsvm/.__init__.py: Package root; exports FeatureMap, QVC, QKE

configs/.default.yaml: Full hyperparameter config (see config schema section)

scripts/.train_qke.py: CLI entrypoint — QKE kernel estimation + SVM

tests/.test_feature_map.py: Unit tests for circuit construction and statevector output
tests/.test_kernel.py: Tests: K(x,x)=1, K symmetric, K PSD
tests/.test_qvc.py: Tests: cost function range, SPSA step execution
tests/.test_qke.py: Tests: SVM convergence, support vector extraction

public_interface.description: Initialise feature map; reps=2 gives the double-U_Phi structure
Return Qiskit QuantumCircuit for U_Phi(x)H^n U_Phi(x)H^n. x shape: [n_qubits]
Return dict mapping subset S -> phi_S(x). For n=2: {1}->x1, {2}->x2, {1,2}->(pi-x1)(pi-x2)
Execute circuit and return complex statevector [2^n]. Used for exact kernel in simulation.

implementation_notes: U_Phi diagonal gate for |S|=1: RZ(2*phi_{i}(x)) on qubit i
U_Phi diagonal gate for |S|=2: ZZ-coupling via CNOT-RZ-CNOT (Fig 1c decomposition)
Circuit structure: H^n -> U_Phi(x) -> H^n -> U_Phi(x)
Expose circuit without measurement for kernel composition

public_interface.signature: (feature_map: FeatureMap, backend: AerSimulator, shots: int = 1024, use_statevector: bool = True)
public_interface.description: shots ignored if use_statevector=True (exact). use_statevector=True for simulation reproducibility.
Compute K(x,z) = |<Phi(x)|Phi(z)>|^2. Exact: inner product of statevectors. Shot-based: count |0...0> from U†_Phi(x) U_Phi(z) |0^n>.
Compute full kernel matrix K[i,j]=evaluate(X[i],Y[j]). If Y=None, symmetric training matrix. Shape: [len(X), len(Y)].
Private: compose U_Phi(x)^dag . U_Phi(z), append measurement. Transpile to backend.
Clip negative eigenvalues to epsilon; return nearest PSD matrix.

implementation_notes: EXACT mode (default for simulation): statevector inner product — no shot noise
SHOT mode (hardware fidelity): apply U†_Phi(x) U_Phi(z) to |0^n>, count |0...0> over `shots` repetitions
K(x,x) must equal 1.0 exactly (sanity check in unit tests)
Kernel matrix is symmetric: compute upper triangle, reflect
Paper used 50,000 shots per entry on hardware; default shots=1024 for simulation speed

public_interface.signature: (feature_map: FeatureMap, depth: int, backend: AerSimulator, shots: int = 1024)
public_interface.description: depth=l sets number of W(theta) entangling layers. theta dim = 2*n_qubits*(depth+1).
Return full circuit: U_Phi(x) -> W(theta). W = U^(l)_loc U_ent ... U^(1)_loc. No measurement.

public_interface.signature: (x: np.ndarray, theta: np.ndarray, b: float = 0.0) -> Dict[int, float]
public_interface.description: Execute circuit R times; return {+1: p_hat_plus, -1: p_hat_minus} via parity measurement f=Z1Z2.

public_interface.signature: (x: np.ndarray, theta: np.ndarray, b: float = 0.0) -> int
public_interface.description: Decision rule: return y = argmax p_hat_y, with bias b.

public_interface.signature: (theta: np.ndarray, X_train: np.ndarray, y_train: np.ndarray, b: float, R_cost: int = 200) -> float
public_interface.description: Compute R_emp(theta) using sigmoid approximation to binomial CDF (EQ8). Returns scalar in [0,1].

public_interface.signature: (X_train: np.ndarray, y_train: np.ndarray, n_iter: int = 250) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, float, List[float]]
public_interface.description: Run SPSA for n_iter steps. Returns (theta_star, b_star, cost_history).

public_interface.signature: (X_test: np.ndarray, y_test: np.ndarray, theta: np.ndarray, b: float = 0.0) -> float
public_interface.description: Classification success rate on test set.

implementation_notes: W(theta) layers: alternate U_loc^(t)(theta_t) and U_ent
U_loc^(t) = tensor product of SU(2) rotations: exp(i/2 theta_z Z) exp(i/2 theta_y Y) per qubit
U_ent = product of CZ gates along linear chain (or device connectivity edge set E)
Parity measurement f(z) = (-1)^(z[0]+z[1]) for n=2; maps bitstrings to {+1,-1}
p_hat_y = count(bitstrings where f(z)=y) / shots
Sigmoid approx: sig(sqrt(R)*(0.5*(1+y*b) - p_hat_y) / sqrt(2*(1-p_hat_y)*p_hat_y))
SPSA: theta_{k+1} = theta_k - a_k * (cost(theta+c_k*delta) - cost(theta-c_k*delta)) / (2*c_k) * delta^{-1}
b is optimized jointly as extra parameter appended to theta vector

public_interface.description: C is SVM regularization. Paper uses hard margin (no slack implied by 100% separable data), but C=1.0 is safe default.
Compute K_train = kernel_estimator.build_kernel_matrix(X_train), enforce PSD, then fit sklearn SVC(kernel='precomputed'). Store support vectors, alpha_i, bias b.
For each test point s: compute K(x_i, s) for support vectors x_i, return sign(sum_i y_i alpha*_i K(x_i,s) + b).
Classification success rate.
Returns (support_vectors, alpha_i, y_sv) matching paper Table S2 format.
Return raw sum_i y_i alpha*_i K(x_i, s) + b for each test point (for Fig 3b bottom panel).

implementation_notes: Use sklearn.svm.SVC(kernel='precomputed', C=C) — wrap precomputed K_train matrix
For inference kernel K_test: shape [len(X_test), len(X_train_support)] — sklearn handles this
Paper reports support vectors explicitly in Table S2: verify alpha_i values match (within sampling noise)
bias b computed from KKT: b = y_i - sum_j y_j alpha_j K(x_j, x_i) for any support vector i

public_interface.description: Richardson extrapolation with given noise scale factors. Paper uses [1.0, 1.5].
Richardson first-order: E_mitigated = (s2*E1 - s1*E2) / (s2 - s1) for two scale factors.

public_interface.signature: (circuit: QuantumCircuit, observable_fn: Callable, noise_model: NoiseModel) -> float
public_interface.description: Run circuit under noise_model at each scale_factor; call mitigate_expectation.

implementation_notes: In simulation: approximate noise scaling by adjusting depolarizing error rate p -> s*p
Paper stretches physical gate times; in simulation inject depolarizing channel with scaled error
For ideal (noiseless) simulation: ZNE module is a no-op passthrough
conf=0.72 — make this component optional via config flag `use_error_mitigation: bool`
TODO: verify Richardson formula matches paper's zero-noise extrapolation implementation

public_interface.signature: (n_per_label: int = 20, gap: float = 0.3, seed: int = 42, n_qubits: int = 2)
public_interface.description: Generate synthetic dataset. gap=Delta=0.3 as per paper.
Return (X, y): X shape [2*n_per_label, n_qubits] ∈ (0,2π]^2, y ∈ {+1,-1}. Uses random V ∈ SU(4) (seeded).
Sample random unitary V ∈ SU(4) via scipy.stats.unitary_group. Shape [4,4] complex128.

public_interface.signature: (x: np.ndarray, V: np.ndarray, feature_map: FeatureMap, gap: float) -> Optional[int]
public_interface.description: Return +1/-1 if |<Phi(x)|V†(Z1Z2)V|Phi(x)>| >= gap, else None (reject — white region in Fig 3b).

public_interface.signature: (test_n_per_label: int = 20, seed: int = None) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
public_interface.description: Return X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test with disjoint draws.

implementation_notes: Rejection sampling: draw x ∈ (0,2π]^2 uniformly; compute expectation; accept if |val| >= gap
V is fixed per dataset instantiation (seeded); ensures reproducible decision boundary
Z1Z2 operator: diagonal matrix with entries (-1)^(z1+z2) in computational basis
Expectation ⟨Φ(x)|V†(Z1Z2)V|Φ(x)⟩ = statevector† @ V† @ Z1Z2_matrix @ V @ statevector

public_interface.description: Fraction of correct predictions.
Aggregate mean + std of success rates per depth (for Fig 3c reproduction).
Set numpy + random seeds.

public_interface.signature: (K: np.ndarray, K_ideal: Optional[np.ndarray], title: str) -> Figure
public_interface.description: Reproduce Fig 4a heatmap.

public_interface.signature: (classifier, X_train, y_train, support_vectors, X_test, y_test, resolution: int = 100) -> Figure
public_interface.description: Reproduce Fig 3b.
Reproduce Fig 3a.
Reproduce Fig 3c with both methods.

tensor_flows.name: QKE Forward Pass — Kernel Entry Computation
QKE Forward Pass — Kernel Entry Computation (shot mode)
steps: circuit = U†_Phi(x_i) composed with U_Phi(x_j), prepend |0^n⟩ init
transpile circuit to backend gate set

tensor_flows.name: QKE Inference — Single Test Point
steps: K_test_row = [evaluate(sv_i, s) for sv_i in support_vectors]  → [N_sv] float64
decision = sum(y_i * alpha*_i * K_test_row[i]) + b_star       → scalar

tensor_flows.name: QVC Training — SPSA Step
steps: b: scalar float64  (bias, appended as extra param)
circuit = U_Phi(x_i) composed with W(theta)  → QuantumCircuit
run circuit R=200 shots → counts [4] int
R_emp = mean(cost_i over training set)  → scalar float64

tensor_flows.name: QVC Inference — Single Point
steps: circuit = U_Phi(x) composed with W(theta_star)  → QuantumCircuit
label = +1 if p_hat_plus > p_hat_minus else -1  (b*=0 at inference)

data.comment_V: ASSUMED: fixed seed for reproducibility — conf=0.80

spsa.comment: ASSUMED: Spall canonical defaults — conf=0.55 — SWAP FREELY

error_mitigation.comment: ASSUMED: disabled for ideal simulation — conf=0.65
error_mitigation.comment_rate: ASSUMED: approximate hardware error rate — conf=0.60

rationale.qiskit-aer: Local statevector and noisy simulation backends
rationale.scikit-learn: SVC with precomputed kernel for QKE; metrics
rationale.numpy: Matrix ops, statevector arithmetic, linear algebra
rationale.scipy: scipy.stats.unitary_group for random V ∈ SU(4); optional QP solver
rationale.tqdm: Progress bars for kernel matrix computation (40x40 = 1600 entries)

entrypoints.description: Train and evaluate Quantum Variational Classifier across all depths

cli_args.help: Single depth to run; if None runs all [0..4]

entrypoints.description: Build quantum kernel matrix and train SVM; evaluate on test sets

cli_args.help: I, II, or III; if None runs all

entrypoints.description: Full paper reproduction — runs QVC + QKE, generates all plots
Load saved results and regenerate all paper figures

docker_spec.rationale: No GPU needed — CPU statevector simulation sufficient for n=2 qubits

risk_assessment.description: Gain sequence parameters (a, c, A, alpha, gamma) not given in paper. Wrong values cause non-convergence or oscillation.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose all SPSA params in config.yaml with Spall defaults. Implement learning rate sweep. Make SPSA swappable with COBYLA/ADAM via config flag `optimizer: spsa|adam|cobyla`.

risk_assessment.description: Paper stretches physical gate times; in simulation we inject depolarizing noise at scaled rates. Approximation may not match paper's ZNE behavior.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default config sets `error_mitigation.enabled: false`. Flag as optional. Document that ideal simulation results should match paper's ideal (dashed) curves, not experimental curves.

risk_assessment.component: Random unitary V for data generation
risk_assessment.description: Paper's specific V ∈ SU(4) is unknown; different V produces different decision boundaries (still classifiable but visually different from Fig 3b).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Fix seed; document that boundary shape will differ. Unit test: verify 100% classification success (not visual match) as correctness criterion.

risk_assessment.description: In exact statevector mode kernel is exactly PSD. In shot mode negative eigenvalues can occur. sklearn may raise if K not PSD.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Always call enforce_psd() before passing to sklearn. Unit test: assert min eigenvalue >= -1e-10 after enforcement.

risk_assessment.description: Paper's Fig 3a shows specific convergence curves. Exact replication requires identical SPSA trajectory which is stochastic.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Report mean ± std over 3 datasets per depth. Correctness criterion: depth=4 should reach >90% success on simulation.
